skill: Inference from sample statistics and margin of error
stem: A factory packages bags of coffee beans, each intended to contain approximately 17 ounces. To check the accuracy of the packaging, 275 bags were randomly selected and weighed. Based on the sample, it is estimated that the average weight of all bags packaged in a 6-hour shift is 17.5 ounces, with an associated margin of error of 0.22 ounces. Which of the following is the most appropriate conclusion?

answerOptions.content: The average weight of all bags is exactly 17.5 ounces.
Plausible values for the average weight of all bags are less than 17.28 ounces or greater than 17.72 ounces.
Plausible values for the average weight of all bags are between 17.28 ounces and 17.72 ounces.
The average weight of all bags is greater than 17.5 ounces.

rationale:
Choice C is correct. The plausible range for the population mean is 17.5 ± 0.22, i.e., 17.28 to 17.72 ounces.

Choice A treats the sample mean as exact. Choice B inverts the plausible interval. Choice D draws an unsupported one-sided conclusion.
